items.title: The National | Allies reject Trump’s call for help in Hormuz
items.summary_auto: March 16, 2026 | NATO allies reject Trump’s demand for help re-opening the crucial Strait of Hormuz. A powerful storm brings 40 centimetres of snow to parts of northern Ontario and Quebec. And, a recruitment campaign brings hundreds of doctors and nurses to B.C.

items.title: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of deadly strike on Kabul hospital that it says killed hundreds
items.summary_auto: Afghanistan accused Pakistan of targeting a hospital for drug users in the Afghan capital late Monday, saying the airstrike had killed at least 400 people. It marked a dramatic escalation of a conflict that began late last month and has seen repeated cross-border clashes as well as airstrikes inside Afghanistan. International calls for a ceasefire have gone unheeded.

items.title: B.C. hires more than 400 U.S. health-care workers in 1-year recruitment blitz
items.summary_auto: The numbers from B.C.’s Ministry of Health show more than 400 U.S. health-care workers accepted jobs in the province between March 2025 and January 2026.

items.title: Trump expects to have the 'honour' of taking Cuba
items.summary_auto: Calling Cuba a 'failed nation' with a 'nice landscape,' U.S. President ‌Donald Trump said Monday that ⁠he thinks ‌he will have the 'honour' of ⁠taking ⁠Cuba, adding he could do anything he wants with it.

items.title: Canadian Para hockey trailblazer Raphaëlle Tousignant's dual fight for health, women's equality
items.summary_auto: Raphaëlle Tousignant has spent her life beating the odds, first as a survivor of childhood bone cancer and later as a trailblazing Para hockey player.

items.title: Team Canada's jerseys for the FIFA World Cup are here
items.summary_auto: The jerseys Team Canada will wear for the FIFA World Cup later this year have been revealed. The home jersey is a iconic Canadian red with a two tone maple leaf with a split in the middle. The away jersey is black with white cracked ice patterns which form maple leaves.

items.title: Why does a Middle East oil blockage affect Canadian gas prices? | About That
items.summary_auto: Andrew Chang explains how Iran's blockage of the Strait of Hormuz — a critical oil-shipping route in the Middle East — affects the cost of energy here in Canada. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters and Getty Images

items.title: The broken economic argument for Trump’s war on Iran | About That
items.summary_auto: Andrew Chang explains the economic impact of the U.S.-Israel war against Iran and how it contradicts President Donald Trump's claim that rising oil prices benefit the U.S. economy. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters, Adobe Stock and Getty Images

items.title: Global oil prices continue to soar, impacting costs for Canadians
items.summary_auto: The price of crude oil continues to rise amid the U.S.-Israel war with Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, increasing costs at home for Canadians.

items.title: Cuban officials report country-wide blackout amid U.S. energy blockade
items.summary_auto: The Ministry of Energy and Mines on X noted a "complete disconnection" of the country's electrical system and said it was investigating.

items.title: EU shows 'no appetite' to expand naval mission in Mideast
items.summary_auto: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas says despite U.S. President Donald Trump's appeal for other countries to help police the Hormuz Strait, there is "no appetite" to change the EU's current mission in the region, which consists of a fleet patrolling the Red Sea to protect transiting ships from attacks by Yemen’s Houthi rebel group.

items.title: Slain Iran critic said months ago that murder suspects wanted to poison him
items.summary_auto: Maple Ridge resident Mehdi Ahmadzadeh Razavi, 48, and North Vancouver resident Arezou Soltani, 45, are charged with first-degree murder in the killing of 45-year-old Masood Masjoody.

items.title: Susie Wiles, Trump's chief of staff, diagnosed with breast cancer but plans to keep working
items.summary_auto: Susie Wiles, the first woman to serve as White House chief of staff, has been diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer but will continue working ‌while undergoing treatment, U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday.

items.title: International student from India killed in Fort St. John, B.C.
items.summary_auto: Gurkirat Singh Manocha, who was studying in Fort St. John from central India, was killed in an "altercation" at a boat launch Friday night, police say.

items.title: Why the U.S. is sending 2,500 marines to the Gulf — and what they might do there
items.summary_auto: While U.S. President Donald Trump tries to convince allies to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, he is also sending 2,500 marines to the region — the first deployment of U.S. troops since it and and Israel first attacked Iran on Feb. 28.

items.title: Thousands of flights grounded as storms bring heavy snow, tornado risks to U.S. East, Midwest
items.summary_auto: Chaotic weather coast to coast in the U.S. — from unusual heat in California to damaging winds around Washington, D.C. — put more than 100 million people in the path of extreme conditions on Monday.

items.title: Pikangikum First Nation leaders say OPP mishandled recent school threat incident
items.summary_auto: The Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) has responded to concerns from community leaders in Pikangikum First Nation about the way officers handled a recent incident involving a school threat. Here's what we know about what happened in the remote Ojibway community in northwestern Ontario.

items.title: Alberta to host 2028 World Cup of Hockey as tournament returns after 12 years
items.summary_auto: The fourth World Cup of Hockey will take place in February 2028 in Alberta, the NHL announced.

items.title: Sniper at St. Patrick's Day street party in Waterloo, Ont., a tactical officer who was part of emergency plan
items.summary_auto: The Waterloo Regional Police Service says the sniper photographed at the St. Patrick's Day street party in Waterloo, Ont., on Saturday is one of their tactical officers.

items.title: Cardi B calls out Canadian fans for not selling out her Hamilton tour date
items.summary_auto: The award-winning rapper says while many tour dates in other cities including Toronto and Vancouver have sold out or are close to it, her concert in Hamilton, Ont., is only about 80 per cent full.

items.title: Stranded footwear and stuffed warehouses: How the Middle East conflict is hitting China’s economy
items.summary_auto: For a snapshot of how the U.S.-led war in the Persian Gulf is affecting global trade and supply chains, look at Yiwu, China. Shipping containers of household goods are backing up as logistics come under pressure.

items.title: Ottawa puts $200M into space launch pad in Nova Scotia
items.summary_auto: The federal government is putting $200 million toward a Canadian-owned launch pad to send satellites into orbit.

items.title: Much of B.C. under weather warnings for snowfall, rain and winter storms
items.summary_auto: Dozens of weather alerts have been issued, with heavy rain forecast for parts of Metro Vancouver and Vancouver Island, and snow expected in much of Interior and northern B.C.

items.title: Bus driver in Laval, Que., daycare crash deemed a high-risk accused
items.summary_auto: Pierre Ny St-Amand, the man who drove a bus into a Laval, Que., daycare in 2023, killing two young children and injuring six others, has been declared a high-risk accused.

items.title: Sudbury mayor says 2-day storm 'unprecedented' as snow blankets northeastern Ontario
Canada's annual inflation rate fell to 1.8% in February, war's impact not yet reflected
items.summary_auto: Canada's annual inflation rate fell to 1.8 per cent in February, Statistics Canada said on Monday.

items.title: Sides in WNBA labour talks report progress but work remains on 'big items'
items.summary_auto: Both the WNBA and players' union feel progress is being made toward a new collective bargaining agreement, but they both say there's still work to be done to get a deal to the finish line.

items.title: Teen sentenced to 3 years for lighting Saskatoon high school classmate on fire
items.summary_auto: The teen who lit her classmate on fire at a Saskatoon high school has been sentenced to three years, the maximum sentence available under the Youth Criminal Justice Act for the crime of attempted murder.

items.title: 'Long overdue’ remembrance: WW I veteran's name to go on Guelph, Ont., cenotaph 100 years after death
items.summary_auto: Ranald (Big Mac) Macdonald of Guelph, Ont., fought in the First World War but his name doesn't appear on the city's cenotaph. Now, it will be added to the Guelph War Memorial with a small ceremony expected before Remembrance Day.

items.title: Canada joins allies in warning against Israeli ground offensive in Lebanon
items.summary_auto: "The humanitarian situation ⁠in Lebanon, including ongoing mass displacement, is already deeply alarming," said a statement by ​Canada, France, Britain, Germany and Italy.

items.title: Trump upset as key U.S. partners shun call for Hormuz warship escorts
items.summary_auto: Israel said on Monday it had detailed plans for at least three more weeks of war as it pounded sites across Iran overnight while Iranian drone attacks temporarily shut the Dubai airport and hit a key oil facility in the United Arab Emirates.

items.title: This Ontario mom nearly died of sepsis. Researchers say cases like hers are overlooked
Back to the fax? Doctors say Ottawa's plans to axe prescription software leaves them in limbo
items.summary_auto: A pair of Ontario family doctors say they may have to go back to sending patient prescriptions to pharmacies by fax because a federally funded agency is doing away with an electronic system, with no clear plans for its replacement.

items.title: Why do electric flashes keep breaking Ottawa's LRT?
items.summary_auto: Electric 'arcing' has shut down the LRT before, but OC Transpo thought it had a fix — until last week.

items.title: This Sudbury, Ont., man creates website to track northern Ontario's highway collisions
items.summary_auto: Following several fatal collisions in northern Ontario this winter, a Sudbury, Ont., resident has created a website to track incidents across the region. Northern Road Watch offers real-time highway incident tracking by compiling data from several different sources — such as Ontario 511 and Environment and Climate Change Canada — into one interactive map. Here's what he hopes the project accomplishes.

items.title: Builders say Toronto World Cup traffic plan could stall projects and delay new homeowners
items.summary_auto: As Toronto prepares to welcome hundreds of thousands of FIFA World Cup fans this summer, thousands of new homeowners could soon get some bad news because the city has severely limited construction activity downtown and along major arteries between May 1 and July 31.

items.title: Life-threatening complications overlooked in weeks after childbirth, researchers say
items.summary_auto: New Canadian research suggests close to a third of life-threatening complications linked to pregnancy and childbirth happen to women during the first six weeks following a delivery — a time when mothers typically experience far less medical tracking and support than they did during pregnancy.
